title: Review and setlist: Dropkick Murphys fires up crowd, flames ICE at St. Paddy’s Day kickoff show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys performed at MGM Music Hall at Fenway, kicking off their St. Patrick's Day celebrations on March 13, 2026.
The band delivered a mix of classics and new songs while addressing political issues, including a protest against ICE.
The concert featured support from Boston punk bands The Vigilantes, Haywire, and Showcase Showdown as opening acts.

title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, from New Braintree, Mass., is running the Boston Marathon for Team Beth Israel Lahey Health.
Bennett, diagnosed with Type 1 diabetes at age 11, aims to support Joslin Diabetes Center and raise awareness.
He runs to inspire others with diabetes, including the 11-year-old version of himself who thought running was impossible.

title: Springfield native Ruth E. Carter keeps making Oscars history
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Ruth E. Carter, from Springfield, has been nominated for her fifth Oscar for costume design in 'Sinners'.
Carter is the most-nominated Black woman in Oscar history and the first Black person to win in Best Costume Design.
If Carter wins on Sunday, she will be the first Black person to win three Oscars, potentially in a self-designed gown.

title: Brookline schools would see more than 200 layoffs if override fails, officials say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Brookline school officials warn of over 200 layoffs if a proposed property-tax override fails to pass.
Schools need $18.5 million over three years to avoid cutting music, language courses, and increasing class sizes.
Brookline voters will decide on the property-tax override at the May 5 annual town election.

title: What to know about the PETS Act aimed at protecting dogs and cats in Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Massachusetts considers the PETS Act to improve animal welfare and regulate pet sales, targeting puppy mill practices.
The bill proposes banning pet shop sales of dogs and cats, encouraging partnerships with shelters for adoptions.
The PETS Act also addresses housing and insurance barriers, promoting broader pet access in public housing.

title: As snow melts, drought still a big issue for Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: State officials urge water conservation in Massachusetts amid intensified drought conditions despite heavy February snowfall.
Central and Northeast Massachusetts elevated to Level 3 Critical Drought, while Connecticut River Valley is at Level 2 Significant Drought.
Snowmelt expected to provide only 2.5 inches of water, insufficient to restore depleted groundwater and surface water levels.

title: Why some women are hiding their boyfriends online
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Chanté Joseph's essay discusses why some women are choosing not to prominently feature boyfriends online.
The essay explores changing attitudes toward relationship dynamics and the role of heterosexuality in women's identities.
Joseph's work sparked significant debate, inspiring art and music, and resulted in a podcast discussion.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for a camp ‘where illness takes a back seat to fun’
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Griffin O’Neill, 27, is running the Boston Marathon to support The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp.
The camp provides children with serious medical conditions a space for fun and friendship in a safe environment.
O’Neill's motivation is personal, inspired by his fiancée’s sister who benefited from the camp during her illness.

title: Eagles reportedly ‘will not trade A.J. Brown at this time’ amid Patriots’ continued interest
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Philadelphia Eagles will not trade A.J. Brown at this time despite interest from the New England Patriots.
Trade discussions for A.J. Brown may resume near June 1 when salary cap relief becomes available for Philadelphia.
The Patriots signed WR Romeo Doubs to a four-year contract, possibly reducing urgency for trading for A.J. Brown.

title: Jayson Tatum set to return vs. Wizards after injury management off-day
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum to play against the Wizards after missing a game for Achilles injury management.
Tatum is easing back with a minutes restriction after a ruptured Achilles, scoring 24 points in his last game.
Baylor Scheierman and Derrick White are probable for Boston; Nikola Vucevic remains out with a finger fracture.
